prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency. Analyze this locksmith's contact/emergency page.

Critical question: can a locked-out person get help within 30 seconds of landing on this page?

HOT LEAD signals:
- Phone number not prominent or not click-to-call
- No service area map or list
- Contact form as primary contact method (useless in emergency)
- No response time stated
- No after-hours information

COLD LEAD signals:
- Giant click-to-call button
- Service area map with coverage zones
- Response time guarantee ("30 min or less")
- 24/7 clearly stated with emergency number
- Live chat
- WhatsApp/text option
- Address with embedded map

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of contact/emergency access>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = harder to reach = better lead>",
  "hasClickToCall": true/false,
  "hasServiceAreaMap": true/false,
  "hasResponseTime": true/false,
  "has24x7Info": true/false,
  "hasLiveChat": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<what a web agency could sell here>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

---
You are analyzing web pages for the category described above.
For each page provided below, extract one record.
Follow the JSON schema from the category description exactly.

## Required fields (always include)
- "url": the page URL
- "pageName": short human-readable page name
- "summary": 1-2 sentence plain-language overview

## Field naming conventions (follow strictly)
- Scores: name ends with "Score", value is a number 1-10 (e.g. "conversionScore": 7)
- Booleans: name starts with "has"/"is" or ends with "Visible" (e.g. "pricingVisible": true)
- Short text: "notes", "description" — 1-2 sentences max
- Tags/lists: arrays of short strings (e.g. "classStyles": ["Hatha", "Vinyasa"])
- Plain strings: everything else (e.g. "price": "€15 drop-in")

Return a JSON object: { "records": [<one record per page>] }.
No markdown, no code fences.
response:
{
  "records": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.banham.co.uk/contact/",
      "pageName": "Banham Contact Us",
      "summary": "The page offers multiple contact routes including phone numbers for existing customers, new enquiries, and a dedicated 24/7 emergency locksmith line (020 7622 5151), but buries the emergency number mid-page among many other query categories. The primary layout pushes users toward lengthy dropdown-driven contact forms rather than surfacing the emergency number immediately at the top.",
      "opportunityScore": 5,
      "hasClickToCall": true,
      "hasServiceAreaMap": false,
      "hasResponseTime": false,
      "has24x7Info": true,
      "hasLiveChat": false,
      "topOpportunity": "Redesign the emergency contact UX: a sticky top-of-page click-to-call banner for the 24/7 locksmith line, plus a clear service area map and stated response time guarantee to convert locked-out visitors before they bounce.",
      "notes": "Banham has the right ingredients (24/7 line, multiple numbers) but hides the emergency CTA deep in a cluttered form-heavy layout — a focused emergency landing page with a prominent click-to-call and response time promise would meaningfully lift conversion."
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.banham.co.uk/showrooms",
      "pageName": "Banham Showrooms",
      "summary": "The page lists four London/South England showrooms with addresses and click-to-call phone numbers, an interactive Google Map with pin markers, and a postcode checker to confirm local service availability. However, it is focused on in-person consultations rather than emergency locksmith response, with no 24/7 or response-time information present.",
      "opportunityScore": 4,
      "hasClickToCall": true,
      "hasServiceAreaMap": true,
      "hasResponseTime": false,
      "has24x7Info": false,
      "hasLiveChat": false,
      "topOpportunity": "Add an emergency callout CTA section above the fold — a prominent 24/7 locksmith phone button with response time promise — to capture locked-out visitors who land on the showrooms page outside business hours.",
      "notes": "Strong location and map presence makes this a good prospect for adding an emergency overlay or sticky banner; the postcode checker is a solid foundation but the page completely ignores after-hours emergency needs."
    }
  ]
}
